$comment: Canonical cross-repo known-answer test for the WS/browser media datagram MAC. Shared byte-for-byte between Onyx Server (src/substrate/cadence_frame.zig test 'ws-media MAC cross-repo KAT') and the Onyx client (src/lib/cadence-media/mediaMac.test.ts). Any change to the derivation or tag MUST update both repos in lockstep.

scheme.transport: browser<->browser media rides binary WebSocket frames on the IRC WSS; server is an SFU/relay that lenient-verifies the sender MAC and forwards the binary frame verbatim to the channel's other on-node call members
scheme.frame: cadence_frame wire format, optionally followed by a 16-byte MAC tag (identical to the native UDP path)
scheme.derive_key: K32 = HMAC-SHA256(key = HMAC-SHA256(key='onyx native-media mac extract v1', msg=root16), msg = 'onyx native-media datagram mac v1' || 0x00 || channel || 0x00 || participant || 0x01)
scheme.key_issuance: server derives K32 from its per-process root + (channel, participant) and hands it to the owning participant at MEDIA JOIN over the authenticated WS: ':server EVENT <nick> MEDIA MACKEY <#chan> <base64 K32>'. The browser HMACs with its issued K32; the server re-derives to verify (stateless).
